skill.name: when-managing-multiple-repos-use-github-multi-repo
skill.description: Coordinates multi-repository GitHub workflows for dependency analysis, cross-repo changes, migration planning, architectural standards, and synchronized releases.
skill.summary: Coordinate GitHub changes across multiple related repositories.
skill.author: DNYoussef

security_audit.summary: Static external-command findings are partially valid because the skill tells agents to run npx, bash scripts, Graphviz, tests, and MCP commands. The weak-cryptography and system-reconnaissance alerts are false positives from prose and Markdown context, and no malicious prompt injection or credential exfiltration was found.

medium_findings.title: External CLI Execution in Repository Workflows
medium_findings.description: The skill directs users or agents to run npx, bash scripts, Graphviz, tests, and MCP commands. This is legitimate for DevOps work, but it can execute local or downloaded tooling with repository access.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible in Markdown examples and task instructions. They appear operational rather than malicious, but they still create real execution risk.

medium_findings.title: Automated Remote Repository Mutation
medium_findings.description: The workflows include cloning repositories, creating branches, opening pull requests, creating repositories, configuring CI, and synchronized releases. Misuse could change remote repositories at scale.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instructions explicitly describe push, PR, release, repository creation, and CI setup workflows. The risk depends on granted GitHub permissions.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Fences Reported as Ruby Backticks
low_findings.description: The repeated backtick findings are Markdown code fences and inline code references, not Ruby backtick execution. The file is documentation for workflows, not a Ruby program.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line context shows fenced Markdown examples and reference links. No Ruby source file or Ruby execution context is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: The reported lines contain skill description, architecture prose, templates, or reference names. No cryptographic function, hashing implementation, or weak cipher use was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matching lines are natural language or file references. I did not find evidence of cryptographic code in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The reported line advises respecting dependency order in a repository graph. It is project dependency analysis, not host reconnaissance or system enumeration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is a best-practice note for cross-repository dependency sequencing. No host, network, or account enumeration command is present there.

dangerous_patterns.title: Unpinned Alpha Package Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill uses npx claude-flow@alpha in command examples. Alpha tags can change over time and should be reviewed before execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The npx commands are explicit and use an alpha distribution tag. This is a supply-chain concern, although no malicious package behavior is shown.

dangerous_patterns.title: Batch Repository Automation Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill repeatedly invokes scripts for cloning, pull requests, releases, migrations, remediation, dashboards, and CI setup. These commands need operator review and scoped credentials.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are clearly documented and target multi-repository automation. They are appropriate for the skill purpose but can affect many repositories.

content.value_statement: Multi-repository work often fails when dependencies, tests, and releases are handled separately. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code structured workflows for coordinated GitHub operations.
actual_capabilities: Defines hierarchical agent roles for coordinating multi-repository work.
Provides workflows for cross-repository change propagation and impact analysis.
Outlines synchronized pull request, merge, tag, and release processes.
Guides monorepo to multi-repo migration planning with history preservation.
Describes architecture compliance scanning and automated remediation planning.
Includes a DOT process diagram for multi-repository coordination phases.
limitations: Referenced shell scripts are not included in the skill package.
Requires GitHub access, repository permissions, and compatible MCP tools.
Does not verify commands before execution or enforce approval gates.
Needs human review for destructive, release, and CI configuration changes.

use_cases.title: Propagate API Changes
use_cases.description: Plan and sequence API updates across dependent services, tests, pull requests, and releases.

use_cases.title: Split a Monorepo
use_cases.description: Design repository boundaries, preserve history, refactor imports, and validate new repositories.

use_cases.title: Enforce Architecture Standards
use_cases.description: Coordinate compliance scans, remediation plans, and CI checks across related repositories.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Multi-Repo Change
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to plan a coordinated change across these repositories: <repos>. Identify dependencies, risks, test order, and PR order.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze Change Impact
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to analyze the impact of <change> across <repos>. Produce affected repositories, dependency order, required tests, and release risks.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Monorepo Split
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design a monorepo to multi-repo migration for <monorepo>. Define repository boundaries, shared packages, history strategy, and cutover phases.

prompt_templates.title: Run Architecture Governance
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to create an architecture enforcement workflow for <repos>. Include standards, compliance checks, remediation PRs, CI gates, and rollback planning.

output_examples.input: Plan a breaking API change across auth, billing, and web repositories.
output: Impact summary listing upstream and downstream repositories.
Dependency-ordered update sequence with test checkpoints.
Draft PR and release coordination plan with rollback notes.

output_examples.input: Prepare a monorepo split for services and shared libraries.
output: Repository boundary proposal by service and shared package.
History preservation and import refactoring plan.
Validation checklist for independent builds and integration tests.

output_examples.input: Create an architecture compliance workflow for ten services.
output: Standards definition plan and rule categories.
Compliance scan sequence with severity levels.

best_practices: Review every generated command before running it against real repositories.
Use scoped GitHub credentials and dry runs for broad repository operations.
Apply changes in dependency order and keep rollback plans ready.
anti_patterns: Do not run batch release commands without confirming affected repositories.
Do not grant organization-wide tokens when repository-scoped access is enough.
Do not let generated remediation PRs bypass code review or CI gates.

faq.question: Does this skill include working automation scripts?
faq.answer: No. It references scripts as workflow helpers, but the package only includes documentation and a process diagram.

faq.question: Can it work with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report marks all three tools as supported, but MCP integrations may require local setup.

faq.question: Is this safe to use on production repositories?
faq.answer: Use caution. Review commands, limit credentials, and require approvals before push, PR, CI, or release operations.

faq.question: What repository counts does it target?
faq.answer: The skill describes small coordination for two to five repositories and larger orchestration for ten or more repositories.

faq.question: Does it detect real dependency graphs automatically?
faq.answer: It explains dependency graph workflows, but actual graph generation depends on external scripts or MCP tools.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill is not malicious, but it guides external commands that can modify many repositories if permissions allow.
